entries.reasoning: Illustrative example of the strategy with a clear favourite. Pred 2 (regulation) is the most likely 90' result — a decisive Spain win — while Pred 3 only pays if the game is level after 90, so it targets the modal extra-time draw. The two deliberately differ.

Worked example on a real, recognisable result: the 2022 final. An evenly-matched final carries huge draw mass, so the EV-optimal regulation and extra-time picks can coincide on the modal draw — and the match really did finish 2-2, then 3-3, before Argentina won on penalties.

Canada are clear favorites with superior attacking quality through Jonathan David and Cyle Larin, and the market makes them strong favorites (away 0.569), though they're missing Alphonso Davies and Ismael Kone. South Africa are a disciplined, defensive counter-attacking side that has overperformed, so low-scoring outcomes and draws carry meaningful mass in this nervy first-ever knockout tie for both nations. Several analysts flag a realistic path to extra time and even a South Africa penalty shootout win, so I keep draw probability high and tilt the advance edge to Canada without making it ove

Brazil are clear favorites with Vinicius in superb form and Neymar available, while Japan are missing playmaker Kubo but remain elite defensively, having conceded just three goals in 540 minutes. Japan are organized and dangerous on the counter, so a cagey, low-scoring tie with realistic draw mass is likely. Brazil's superior firepower should edge it, but multiple previews tip a potential extra-time marathon given Japan's resilience.

Germany are heavy favorites but enter off a loss to Ecuador and have shown attacking issues, while Paraguay are deeply defensive with the second-lowest group-stage xG and openly target extra time. The Schlotterbeck loss and Brown doubt slightly weaken Germany's defense, but Paraguay's suspension of Diego Gomez and doubt over Alderete hurt them more. Expect a low-scoring, grind-it-out match with realistic draw mass and elevated chance of extra time, with Germany most likely winning by a tight margin like 1-0 or 2-1.

Both teams are fully fit and attack-minded — Netherlands and Morocco rank top-14 in xG/90, and neither has kept a clean sheet at the tournament, so both-teams-to-score and over 2.5 goals are strongly favored. The Netherlands are a slight de-vigged favorite (~0.41) with superior squad depth, but Morocco's quality and proven knockout pedigree (penalty win vs Spain in 2022) keep them dangerous and elevate draw/extra-time probability. Regulation draws carry meaningful mass given the open, high-scoring nature, and Morocco's shootout reputation narrows the advance gap relative to the 90-minute line.

Norway are slight favorites with Haaland and Odegaard rested and fresh for the knockouts after sitting out the France loss, giving them match-winning quality despite a shaky defense. Ivory Coast are well-organized, hard to break down and dangerous in transition, but their finishing has been a recurring weakness, missing injured right-back Singo though Ndicka returns fit. Bookmaker odds and previews frame this as a genuine coin flip leaning narrowly to Norway, so regulation draws carry strong mass and extra time is a realistic outcome.

France are overwhelming favorites with the tournament's deepest attack (Mbappé, Dembélé, Olise) and a perfect group stage, while Sweden scraped in as a third-placed team and are missing centre-back Isak Hien, leaving their defense vulnerable. However, Sweden's strike pair of Isak and Gyökeres can punish France on the counter, so a France win with a Sweden goal is the most likely outcome. Regulation draw mass is kept moderate given the talent gap, but knockout caution and Sweden's threat keep extra-time probability meaningful at roughly the de-vigged draw price.

Mexico won Group A with three straight clean sheets and home advantage at a packed Estadio Azteca, where they are virtually unbeaten, supporting their de-vigged edge. Ecuador have an elite defense (Pacho, Hincapie, Caicedo) but have struggled to finish all tournament, so this projects as a tight, low-scoring knockout with strong draw mass and a real chance of extra time. Both sides have settled XIs with no fresh injury concerns, and given two stout defenses, scoreless and one-goal margins dominate the distribution. Mexico is favored to edge it, but the small individual-quality gap keeps Ecuado

England are heavy favourites (market ~72% for the win) as Group L winners with Harry Kane in record-breaking form, though their attack has been inconsistent and they face a right-back crisis with Reece James (hamstring) and Jarell Quansah (ankle) both out, forcing Djed Spence to start. DR Congo are a resilient, physical diaspora-built side (Wissa, Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba) making their first-ever knockout appearance with no reported injuries, and Desabre may deploy a defensive 5-3-2, raising modest chances of a low-scoring game or upset. Low-scoring England wins (1-0, 2-0, 2-1) carry most mass, wit

This is widely regarded as the coin-flip of the round: Belgium have the bigger names but a misfiring attack that only clicked against New Zealand, while Senegal are fast in transition yet defensively fragile and missing keeper Edouard Mendy. Market odds (0.437/0.292/0.271) and Belgium's slight quality/depth edge (Doku confirmed to start) tilt advancement modestly their way, but Senegal's goal threat keeps this open. Goals look likely on both ends, so I've given draws realistic mass and kept scorelines open, with meaningful probability of extra time.

USA field their first-choice XI (Pulisic, Balogun, Richards, Adams) at home and are clear favorites, but Bosnia are a streetwise, defensively organized side dangerous on counters and set pieces through Dzeko, so a regulation draw forcing extra time carries realistic mass. The Americans' strong attacking starts but shaky defense point to outcomes like 1-0, 2-0 and 2-1, with low-scoring draws plausible. If it reaches extra time, USA's depth and fitness slightly favor them, though Bosnia's penalty pedigree (beating Wales and Italy on spot kicks) keeps their advancing chance meaningful.

Spain are heavy favourites (de-vigged ~70% to win regulation) as reigning Euro champions and Group H winners, despite winger injury concerns to Yamal and Munoz, with only Baena near full fitness. Austria, in their first knockout tie since 1982 under Rangnick with Alaba and Sabitzer, are organised but defensively shaky after a 3-3 draw with Algeria. Spain's superior quality favours a 1-2 goal regulation win, but knockout caution and Austria's threat keep meaningful draw mass and a ~20% extra-time chance. In extra time, Spain's depth and class still make them likeliest to edge a tight game.

Portugal are moderate favorites with superior attacking depth (Ronaldo, Bruno Fernandes, Leao) and full squad availability, while Croatia rely on an aging but experienced midfield and have looked defensively vulnerable against quality opposition. Both teams have shown low-scoring, cautious form in knockout-style games, so draws carry heavy mass and roughly a 30% chance of extra time. In ET, Portugal's greater firepower and fresher legs give them the edge, but Croatia's tournament pedigree and penalty resilience keep them live to advance.

Switzerland topped Group B unbeaten with a settled, largely fit squad and are narrow favorites, but the de-vigged market and near pick'em advance odds reflect real uncertainty. Algeria attack well (scored in all three group games) yet conceded six goals and kept no clean sheets, with Amoura a fitness doubt, so a Swiss win with Algeria scoring is a plausible shape. Both sides concede regularly, so scores like 2-1, 1-1 and 1-0 carry the most mass. Knockout tension plus Algeria's threat keeps a meaningful chance of a regulation draw and extra time near 28%.

Both sides are low-scoring and defensively organized, with Australia failing to score in two of three group games and Egypt conceding little, so draws carry heavy mass and extra time is a real possibility. Egypt are slight favorites given superior squad quality (Salah, Marmoush), but Salah's hamstring strain and the confirmed loss of left-back Fattouh temper their edge. The market (away 0.393, draw 0.32, home 0.287) points to a tight contest, so I've weighted 0-0, 1-1 and single-goal margins most heavily. Expect a cagey knockout tie that could easily need extra time or penalties.
